evidence: Section 2.4 states: "The importance of a predictor variable in the RF model can be determined by the increase in the prediction error, which results from a random permutation of the values of an explanatory variable." Section 3.3 interprets magnitude and ranking, stating "predictor variables vehicle traffic intensity, time of day, wind direction and heavy-duty vehicle intensity had the strongest influence at the five study sites," but this ranking is based on permutation importance, not impurity importance. The main conclusions about lockdown impacts (21.9-22.3% NO2 reduction, 12.8% PM10 reduction) come from BAU versus observed concentration comparisons with held-out validation (R2=0.72 for NO2, R2=0.35 for PM10), not from variable importance magnitude.
